original_abstract: “Everyone Knows AI”: A Critical Review of Polish Public Opinion on Artificial Intelligence. While awareness of artificial intelligence is steadily rising in Poland, public perception remains characterized by significant misconceptions, with many respondents conflating AI with general automation or physical robotics. This article presents a systematic review of public opinion research conducted between 2017 and 2023, synthesizing data from 12 distinct studies to map the evolution of national sentiment. The analysis yields five primary thematic conclusions: the conceptual understanding of AI, the lived experience of interacting with technological artifacts, the varying levels of social acceptance for intelligent systems, the perceived threat of labor substitutability, and pervasive public confusion. The findings suggest that while Poles are increasingly recognizing the societal role of artificial intelligence, they remain plagued by uncertainty and apprehension. As AI integrates more deeply into the fabric of daily life, these perceptions are expected to undergo further significant shifts, necessitating ongoing academic and social monitoring.
humanized_abstract: Abstract: “Everyone Knows AI”: A Critical Review of Polish Public Opinion on Artificial Intelligence Artificial intelligence (AI) has been increasingly present in the public debate, and its popularity in Poland is growing. However, as many studies have demonstrated, the Poles often misunderstand the term of AI treating it for example as automation or simply as robotics. This paper provides a systematic review of public opinion research on AI in Poland conducted in the years 2017–2023. The author of the article used 12 studies on this topic to reflect the current public opinion concerning AI in Poland. The main results are presented in five key themes: 1) Conceptualization of AI; 2) Human experience with AI technologies; 3) Social acceptance of intelligent systems; 4) Threats related to the replacement of work by machines; 5) Misunderstanding of AI. The main conclusion of the paper is that the Poles have a rather contradictory approach to AI, when they recognise more and more often the importance of AI in the society, at the same time they feel a high level of uncertainty and fear regarding this technology. The Poles will probably have to cope with many challenges related to the development of AI, which in the future will become increasingly present in everyday life.
